EV vs Gas Car Cost Calculator

Compare the total cost of owning an electric vehicle versus a gas car. Enter your driving habits to see which saves more.

FAQ

Are electric cars cheaper to own?

EVs typically cost less to operate ($0.03-0.05/mile vs $0.10-0.15/mile for gas), but have higher purchase prices. Break-even is usually 3-7 years.

How much do you save on fuel with an EV?

The average EV driver saves $800-$1,500 per year on fuel costs compared to a gas car, depending on electricity rates and gas prices.

Are EVs cheaper to maintain?

Yes, EVs have fewer moving parts, no oil changes, and regenerative braking reduces brake wear. Maintenance costs are 30-50% lower.
